item.title: Top 25 Fantasy Books According To Imgur
item.description:
25. The First Law Series - Joe Abercrombie 24. The Inheritance Cycle - Christopher Paolini 23. Monster Hunter International - Larry Correia 22. The Gentlemen Bastards - Scott Lynch
21. Clan Of The Cave Bears - Jean M. Auel 20. PowderMage Trilogy - Brian McLellan 19. The Lightbringer Series - Brent Weeks 18. The Greenbone Saga - Fonda Lee
17. Sandman Slim - Richard Kadrey 16. Malazan Book Of The Fallen - Steven Erikson 15. Mistborn - Brandon Sanderson 14. The Band Series - Nicholas Eames
13. The Furies Of Calderon - Jim Butcher 12. His Dark Materials - Philip Pullman 11. The Dark Tower - Stephen King 10. The Farseer Trilogy - Robin Hobb
9. The Wheel of Time - Robert Jordan 8. The KingKiller Chronicles - Patrick Rothfuss 7. Harry Potter - JK Rowling 6. Dragonriders of Pern - Anne McCaffrey
5. The Way of Kings - Brandon Sanderson 4. The Dresden Files - Jim butcher 3. American Gods - Neil Gaiman 2. Discworld - Terry Pratchett
1. Good Omens - Terry Pratchett & Neil Gaiman Thank you all for for commenting on my last book post, it was a lot of work going through all 1408 comments and telling them up. https://imgur.com/gallery/gteu1BK Here is the sci-fi list https://imgur.com/gallery/Sn13wD9

item.title: Carefully curated, EZ-2-Read Ukraine news with sources, for those lacking time but who want to stay informed
item.description:
The Pentagon claims that Ukraine is now "able and willing" to retake territory from the russian invaders. Ukraine has regained control of Makariv, near Kyiv: https://www.ukrinform.net/rubric-ato/3418214-ukraines-armed-forces-regain-control-of-makariv-near-kyiv.html https://www.facebook.com/CinCAFU/posts/259431339685692 https://www.newsweek.com/ukraine-makarov-russia-1690326 https://www.pravda.com.ua/eng/news/2022/03/2/7327536/ A large part of the success has been due to the difference in timing of operations. Russians lack sufficient equipment to move their armor at night, so they're exposing themselves greatly by moving during the day. Ukraine, on the other hand, has a wealth of western equipment (think night vision and thermal) for conducting operations at night, often with drones. The Ukrainian special forces in particular have excellent night ops capability, many equipped with GPNVG-18 night vision goggles – which cost over $40,000 each. https://taskandpurpose.com/news/ukraine-russia-destroying-tanks-drones/ https://cepa.org/hunting-the-invader-ukraines-special-operations-troops/ There are reports that Ukrainian forces have surrounded Russia's troops in Bucha, Irpin, and Hostomel and cut them off from supplies.
Ukrainian officials said Tuesday their troops have driven Russian forces out of Makariv. It's a significant event strategically, because this will help allow supplies and troops both in and out of the city, as well as facilitate evacuations and humanitarian aid efforts. https://kyivindependent.com/uncategorized/ukrainian-troops-liberate-makariv/ https://www.newsweek.com/ukraine-makarov-russia-1690326 https://www.axios.com/ukraine-claims-makariv-retaken-202e7d83-9676-45d4-ba5e-bc17ad2cac43.html
Much of the city is still contested, but Ukraine has taken back swaths of it, and it is no longer considered under russian control. Ukraine officials say they will effectively have retaken the city by the end of the day. https://www.timesofisrael.com/liveblog_entry/ukraine-official-says-army-to-retake-kherson-from-russians-today/ https://nypost.com/2022/03/25/ukrainian-troops-take-back-parts-of-kherson-from-russian-forces-us/ https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/world/europe/pentagon-says-russia-no-more-in-full-control-of-kherson/articleshow/90452342.cms https://www.deccanherald.com/international/world-news-politics/ukrainians-fighting-to-retake-kherson-now-contested-city-pentagon-1094831.html
Boris Romantschenko survived the Buchenwald, Peenemünde, Dora-Mittelbau and Bergen-Belsen concentration camps as a young man. has not left his apartment for months for fear of contracting Covid-19. Russian attacks ignited a fire in his home, and he died in the fire. https://www.cnn.com/2022/03/21/europe/borys-romanchenko-death-ukraine-intl/index.html https://de.euronews.com/my-europe/2022/03/21/kz-uberlebender-boris-romantschenko-96-in-ukraine-durch-bomben-getotet https://www.reuters.com/world/wwii-holocaust-survivor-killed-ukraines-kharkiv-2022-03-22/
Rest in peace Boris Romantschenko, 1926-2022
The source is a NATO official who spoke with media outlets (anonymously, for obvious reasons, but all of these reports have been very accurate so far). https://news.yahoo.com/nato-official-estimates-40-000-064410374.html https://www.wsj.com/livecoverage/russia-ukraine-latest-news-2022-03-23/card/russia-lost-up-to-40-000-troops-in-ukraine-nato-estimates-xyZjWxinMDHzdeRZvAeD https://time.com/6160174/russian-soldiers-killed-ukraine/
Komsomolskaya Pravda, which is staunchly pro-kremlin, published an article saying 9,861 Russian servicemen had died, exceeding US intelligence estimates of Russian fatalities, but close to what Ukrainian intelligence has claimed. They quickly removed it and claimed it was the result of a hack. https://www.rferl.org/a/komsomolskaya-pravda-soldier-deaths/31764466.html https://www.forbes.com/sites/masonbissada/2022/03/21/500-or-10000-deaths-russian-media-finally-seems-to-report-dire-troop-casualty-numbers-and-then-deletes-them/?sh=7f2780a23d14 https://www.dailymail.co.uk/news/article-10637425/Russia-loses-10-000-men-just-four-weeks-toll-Ukraine-invasion-revealed.html UNCONFIRMED: A Ukrainian official claims they've intercepted russian communications including a summary that estimates Russian losses at 17,265, which is even higher than Ukrainian intelligence estimates (which were about ~15,000 at the time the claim was made).
These numbers are fairly consistent with NATO estimates and other western intelligence estimates. https://www.ukrinform.net/rubric-ato/3440344-russian-forces-lost-about-16400-personnel-575-tanks.html https://twitter.com/KyivIndependent https://kyivindependent.com/
Lt Gen Yakov Rezantsev was killed near Kherson. Good riddance. https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-60807538 https://news.yahoo.com/ukraine-says-russian-general-killed-125620034.html https://www.ndtv.com/world-news/7-russian-generals-killed-in-ukraine-war-so-far-western-official-2844045
Ukraine has "rewards" (USD cash, plus protection and relocation) available to Russian soldiers who surrender themselves and significant pieces of equipment. "Misha" surrendered himself and his tank and was taken into custody by Ukrainian intelligence. He told them that his tank crew mates had fled home and left him behind. https://www.ukrinform.net/rubric-ato/3437016-russian-serviceman-surrenders-tank-to-ukraine-after-kyiv-offers-10000-reward-citizenship-prospects.html https://www.defenseworld.net/news/31652/Russian_Soldier_Surrenders__Hands_Over_Tank_to_Ukrainian_Army_for__10_000_Reward#.Yjowd-rML-g
A photo taken of the event.
More sanctions are still being considered. https://www.unian.net/war/iz-za-sankciy-v-rossii-ostanovil-rabotu-edinstvennyy-proizvoditel-tankov-novosti-vtorzheniya-rossii-na-ukrainu-11753992.html https://www.indiatoday.in/world/russia-ukraine-war/video/lack-of-components-stop-tank-manufacturing-in-russia-1928010-2022-03-22 https://www.newsweek.com/russia-halts-tank-production-due-supply-problems-ukraine-claims-1690388
Intercepted communications also reveal russian soldiers claiming 50% of them are suffering frostbite (Note: This number can't be verified; it is likely exaggerated). There are reports of self-inflicted injuries in attempt to be pulled back from the front. https://www.thedailybeast.com/russian-troops-are-now-turning-on-each-other-its-a-shtshow-one-soldier-is-recorded-telling-another https://nypost.com/2022/03/18/russian-troops-reportedly-shooting-themselves-in-the-legs-to-avoid-fighting/ As for the fragging...
Western intelligence reports that russian colonel Yury Medvedev was run over by his own troops amid anger at mounting losses in Ukraine, according to western intelligence. The injuries proved fatal. https://news.yahoo.com/russian-commander-deliberately-killed-by-his-own-troops-due-to-ukraine-losses-160618503.html https://www.news.com.au/world/europe/russian-reportedly-runs-over-his-commander-with-tank-in-protest/news-story/894fc691dca588b9f4317776855d2e6c https://www.politico.eu/article/russian-brigade-commander-killed-by-his-own-forces/ https://www.thetimes.co.uk/article/russian-commander-run-over-by-tank-deliberately-killed-by-own-troops-ncdw9fmr9
He has apparently left russia, with "no plans to return" https://www.cnbc.com/2022/03/23/putins-top-aide-chubais-reportedly-quits-opposing-ukraine-invasion.html https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-60849918 https://www.reuters.com/world/europe/russian-reformer-chubais-leaves-post-putins-special-representative-source-2022-03-23/
There was lots of speculation as to where he's been. Some outlets reported he had a heart attack (lol) after a meeting with Vladimir Putain but this is unconfirmed. He has reappeared and given a briefing to a roomful of dejected generals. https://www.wionews.com/world/russian-defence-minister-sergei-shoigu-reappears-amid-speculation-over-whereabouts-465800 https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2022/03/26/russian-defence-minister-reappears-after-2-week-absence-a77093 https://www.newsweek.com/russian-defense-minister-sergei-shoigu-missing-war-ukraine-1691379 https://www.timesofisrael.com/liveblog_entry/russia-denies-defense-minister-had-heart-attack-as-he-reappears-after-2-week-absence/ Truth be told, Shoigu is a massively incompetent man who actually rolled back his predecessor Anatoly Serdiukov's attempts to modernize Russia's army (modernizations that angered the oligarchs who were pilfering money). Turns out that really bit them in the ass... And may bite Shoigu specifically; being a russian general is one of the most dangerous occupations in the world, with a massive rate of "accidents" and "suicides" – see below: https://informnapalm.org/en/wp-content/uploads/sites/14/2016/01/gen2.png Shoigu isn't the only notable security/defense official to disappear; others include the head of the National Guard, Viktor Zolotov, chief of the General Staff of the RF Armed Forces, Valery Gerasimov, and others.
Watch it for yourself. (Link goes to the beginning of the most relevant ~90 seconds of the video, but if you have time, please watch the full interview. It gives me hope because among other things, it means that Ukraine has been preparing for this much longer than the world has truly been paying attention.) https://youtu.be/1xNHmHpERH8?t=472
Before making donations, always be sure to read the site for details on where/how the donation money is spent to make sure it aligns with how you want to help Ukraine. Humanitarian aid: https://www.unicef.org/ukraine/en http://r2p.org.ua/en/ https://www.hias.org/ https://www.pah.org.pl/en/ Military aid: https://bank.gov.ua/ua/news/all/natsionalniy-bank-vidkriv-spetsrahunok-dlya-zboru-koshtiv-na-potrebi-armiyi https://www.portmone.com.ua/r3/uk/terminal/index/index/id/118103 Multipurpose (some funds MAY be used by military) aid: https://razomforukraine.org/ https://kse.ua/support/donation/ If you have additional (preferably *better*) places to donate, please let me know so I can check them out before adding to my posts.

item.title: An indesputable fact from Attenborough
item.description: Anyone who believes in indefinite growth on a physically finite planet is either mad or an economist. David Attenborough
item.dc:creator: alferg

item.title: Top 25 SciFi Novels According To Imgur
item.description:
25. Wayfarer Series - Beck Chambers 24. Magic 2.0 - Scott Meyer 23. Parable Of The Sower - Octavia Butler 22. Frankenstein - Mary Shelley
21. The Martian - Andy Weir 20. Armor - John Steakley 19. Arena Mode - Blake Northcott @BlakeNorthcott 18. The Bobiverse - Dennis E. Taylor
17. The Old Man's War - John Scalzi 16. Neuromancer - William Gibson 15. World War Z - Max Brooks 14. Altered Carbon - Richard K. Morgan
13. The Handmaid's Tale - Margaret Atwood 12. Ready Player One - Ernest Cline 11. Red Rising - Pierce Brown 10. The Murderbot Diaries - Martha Wells
9. Snow Crash - Neil Stephenson 8. 1984 - George Orwell 7. Flowers For Algernon - Daniel Keyes 6. Slaughterhouse-Five - Kurt Vonnegut
5. Dune - Frank Harbert 4. Project Hail Mary - Andy Weir 3. The Expanse - James A. Corey 2. Ender's Game - Orson Scott Card
1. The Hitchhiker's Guide To The Galaxy - Douglas Adams Thank you all for commenting on my book post, it was alot of work crawling through all 1408 comments and tallying up the winners above. Quite surprisingly a few of the books presented above overturned higher numbers due to repeated appearances at lower numbers. https://imgur.com/gallery/gteu1BK I'm working on the fantasy list I'll tag it here when I'm done. Edit: https://imgur.com/gallery/LZW9V85
item.dc:creator: Beattywill80

item.title: Josh ignored sex abuse repeatedly as AG
item.description: Josh "Salute to the Nazi's" Hawley repeatedly chastised SCOTUS nominee Jackson for being too soft on sentencing child porn convicts. As an example, she gave one convicted felon a 72 month sentence vs. the maximum 84 month sentence. She followed the rules Federal judges are given. Meanwhile, when Josh was AG in Missouri, he let sex/violent abusers off with probation when jail-time was recommended by the law; let them be involved in their own investigations; let the Catholic Church direct their own self-investigation, and failed to help victims of sexual abuse -preferring instead to focus instead on the prosecutions that would get him headlines. https://ca.movies.yahoo.com/movies/hawley-attacked-ketanji-brown-jackson-133810536.html
item.dc:creator: RetrogradeLlama

item.title: Not bad.
item.description: I disagree with DeSantis and the Republicans on a lot of issues, but this I support wholeheartedly. This doesn’t excuse the bullshit he has done and is still doing, but recognizing good things is just as important as recognizing bad things.
item.dc:creator: CaptainCrackSparrow

item.title: Samuel L. Jackson Finally Received His First-Ever Oscar
item.description:
No actor’s films have made more money in box office history than Samuel L Jackson, but he had never won an Oscar until Friday, when he was awarded an honorary statuette by the Academy. The 73-year-old known for collaborations with Spike Lee and Quentin Tarantino, and a regular feature in blockbusters from Marvel to Star Wars, had previously secured only one Oscar nomination for “Pulp Fiction.”
Jackson was presented with a statuette honoring career achievement at a star-studded Governors Awards gala in Hollywood, just two days before the main Academy Awards in which last year’s films and nominees will compete.
“152 movie titles, $27 billion in box office, more than any other actor in history,” said Denzel Washington, presenting Jackson with the award. Jackson, seated alongside Tarantino in the Dolby Ballroom, took the stage to reminisce on his career, which also includes the original “Jurassic Park” and action sequel “Die Hard with a Vengeance.” “It’s been a real pleasure making indelible impressions on audiences as ‘gang member number two,’ ‘hold-up man’ and ‘unforgettable black guy’ — just to name a few,” he joked.
Jackson wasn’t the only Hollywood icon to be recognized at the Governors Awards. Three other honorees received trophies at the event. Elaine May — a comedic trailblazer and acclaimed writer-director — and actor Liv Ullmann — frequent collaborator of filmmaker Ingmar Bergman — also received Honorary Awards. Likewise, actor Danny Glover received the Jean Hersholt Humanitarian Award. In his statement, Rubin credited Glover’s award to his “decades-long advocacy for justice and human rights reflects his dedication to recognizing our shared humanity on and off the screen.” Much like Jackson, none of these honorees ever won an Oscar before.
item.dc:creator: Yaratam

item.title: The longest-everViking longship was unearthed by accident during renovations of a Danish longship museum. The Oseberg ship (Norwegian: Osebergskipet) is a well-preserved Viking ship discovered in a large burial mound at the Oseberg farm near Tønsberg

item.dc:creator: PUSClFER

item.title: Unhappy jellies
item.description:
The saltwater line for this jelly Kreisel was left slightly open last night, so this morning, when I came in for work, I was welcomed by a nice big pool of water on the ground. More interestingly, though, these little guys were behaving rather strangely. Turns out, the water that was slowly running into the system was around 24 degrees C (75 degrees F), which is significantly warmer than the Kreisels’ baseline temperature, 15 degrees C (59 degrees F). This created a thermocline in the Kreisel where the colder water settled at the bottom and the warmer water stayed at the top. Since the jellies are used to the colder water, they kept trying to swim back down toward it, however, they were unable to because the water in the Kreisel is constantly flowing. The result: jellies swimming upside down.
This is what they would do if the flow rate wasn’t high enough to keep them suspended in the water column. Silly guys.
item.dc:creator: BootyCravinPikmin
